title: colea obtusifolia
intro: Colea obtusifolia, also known as the African boxwood or African box tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic. Colea obtusifolia can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are typically taken orally to harness its medicinal benefits.
